headline: The VIX Hasn’t Been This Calm Since March. Here’s Why Traders Are Breathing Easier.
summary: The CBOE Volatility Index (^VIX) slipped to the 19 level on Wednesday morning, down roughly 2.5%, signaling fear has subsided. Traders and investors appear to be anticipating smoother sailing ahead. President Trump’s decision to extend the ceasefire with Iran has helped, providing a positive catalyst for stocks to move higher. The CBOE Volatility Index (^VIX) ... The VIX Hasn’t Been This Calm Since March. Here’s Why Traders Are Breathing Easier.
